title: Scheduling, Affinity, Taints, Topology, Priority, Preemption, and Eviction · Kubernetes Retrieval
summary: An on-call handoff reconstructs scheduling, affinity, taints, topology, priority, preemption, and eviction from delayed object status, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the placement and disruption decision record under changed capacity. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for placement and disruption decision record after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use trusted node labels, nodeSelector, required and preferred node affinity, operators, weights, and ignored-during-execution semantics.
Design inter-Pod affinity and anti-affinity with selectors, namespaces, topology keys, scheduler cost, and failure-domain consequences.
Trace NoSchedule, PreferNoSchedule, and NoExecute taints, matching tolerations, toleration time, node conditions, and admission-added behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: K8s Node Selector Affinity · changed case · Review K8s Node Selector Affinity
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Pod is Pending with an untolerated taint, required anti-affinity, insufficient memory, and topology spread skew 4. Reconstruct filtering, scoring, requests, selectors, affinity, taints, preemption, and eviction without forcing nodeName.
